titleStmt.title: Telegram from the Secretary of the War Office to the Davidsons, 3 March 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: The Secretary of the War Office

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
This telegram is part of a large correspondence from Captain James Davidson to his parents. Davidson, who was Managing Director of the Sirocco Engineering Works in Belfast, served with 13 Royal Irish Rifles and the Machine Gun Corps. His letters describe his experiences while training in England and serving in France. He was killed on 1 July 1916, the first day of the Battle of the Somme.
This telegram alerts Captain Davidson's parents that he although he was slightly wounded, he remained with his battalion.

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland

correspAction.persName: The Secretary of the War Office

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
POST OFFICE TELEGRAPHS 3 MR 16 OHMS War Office London Handed in at} 7.35 .M., Received here at} 8.27.M TO{ Davidson Seacourt . Bangor Co Down. Beg to report that Captain
J. S. Davidson. Irish Rifles
wounded slightly 29th Feby. remained
at duty. Sentry War Office
March. /16
TELEGRAM.
NO CHARGE FOR DELIVERY. Davidson
Seacourt 3/3/16
